All direct Bible quotes in the faith notes garden now use **NASB 1995** wording. JoRap commentary stays in your voice; only quoted scripture text was changed.

### What changed

**43 gospel / Eternal Principles notes** — paraphrased scripture in `key_concept`, `shareable_thought`, and related fields replaced with NASB 1995 text, for example:

- Matthew Sermon on the Mount cluster (beatitudes, light, treasure, narrow gate, Lord Lord, etc.)
- Romans 12:1–2 (renewed mind, living sacrifice, discern His will)
- Galatians 5 fruit/flesh lists
- Ephesians 2, justification passages, 1 Corinthians 3:12–15, John 15:5, and others

**New reference files**
- `data/scripture-nasb1995.yaml` — canonical NASB 1995 wording by reference
- `scripts/apply-nasb-quotes.py` — migration map + self-check (run after bulk edits)

**jorap-voice skill** — new rule: quote Bible **text** in NASB 1995; cite `data/scripture-nasb1995.yaml`

### Format

Direct quotes use NASB wording, then the reference in parentheses. Commentary around them stays plain JoRap voice:

> *for where your treasure is, there your heart will be also. (Matthew 6:19-21). Earthly treasure is fragile…*

Flashcard backs stay apply-first (not full verse recitation), per the flashcards skill.

Say if you want the same rule applied to blog posts or other content outside the notes garden.

import re

```bash
SPINE = """grace mercy free-grace justification sanctification present-a-living-sacrifice renewed-mind dont-copy-the-pattern discern-his-will-daily christlikeness faith-and-works repent-and-believe childlike-faith abide-in-me judgment-seat loss-of-reward standing-vs-fellowship assurance grateful-obedience faithful-steward success-is-stewardship love-god love-your-neighbor the-golden-rule love-your-enemies forgiveness reconciliation-before-worship peacemakers turn-the-other-cheek let-your-yes-be-yes judge-not by-their-fruits heart-righteousness ask-seek-knock secret-devotion seek-the-kingdom-first god-centered-design dont-worry treasure-in-heaven eternal-rewards the-beatitudes fruits-of-the-spirit discipleship discipleship-vs-leadership humility-and-service let-your-light-shine take-up-your-cross the-wise-builder the-narrow-way christianity-and-politics render-unto-caesar great-commission""".split()
jesus_ref = re.compile(r"\((Matthew|Mark|Luke|John)\s+[\d:]")
missing = []
for slug in SPINE:
    p = ROOT / f"{slug}.md"
    if not p.exists():
        missing.append((slug, "FILE MISSING"))
        continue
    text = p.read_text()
    m = re.search(r"key_concept: \|\n((?:  .+\n)+)", text)
    head = m.group(1)[:600] if m else ""
    if not jesus_ref.search(head):
        missing.append((slug, "no Jesus ref in key_concept head"))
print(f"Spine notes checked: {len(SPINE)}")
for s, why in missing:
    print(f"  MISSING: {s} — {why}")
if not missing:
    print("All spine notes have Jesus refs in key_concept opening.")
PY
```

### Pattern
Each note leads with a direct Jesus quote and reference, for example:

- **Repent and Believe** — Mark 1:15: *The time is fulfilled, and the kingdom of God is at hand; repent and believe in the gospel.*
- **Free Grace** — John 5:24 first; Ephesians/Romans follow as Paul expanding the same ground
- **Fruits of the Spirit** — John 15:8 first; Galatians 5 lists the cluster after
- **Judgment Seat** — Matthew 16:27 first; 1 Corinthians 3 follows for the fire test

Paul and other writers stay where they add detail — they no longer anchor the principle.

- **`scripts/apply-jesus-quotes.py`** — prepends Jesus openers for future spine edits
- **`eternal-principles.md`** index — theological basis bullets now cite Jesus passages
- **jorap-voice skill** — Eternal Principles must anchor on Jesus' words first

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import re
ROOT = Path("/home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/content/english/notes")
SPINE = """grace mercy free-grace justification sanctification present-a-living-sacrifice renewed-mind dont-copy-the-pattern discern-his-will-daily christlikeness faith-and-works repent-and-believe childlike-faith abide-in-me judgment-seat loss-of-reward standing-vs-fellowship assurance grateful-obedience faithful-steward success-is-stewardship love-god love-your-neighbor the-golden-rule love-your-enemies forgiveness reconciliation-before-worship peacemakers turn-the-other-cheek let-your-yes-be-yes judge-not by-their-fruits heart-righteousness ask-seek-knock secret-devotion seek-the-kingdom-first god-centered-design dont-worry treasure-in-heaven eternal-rewards the-beatitudes fruits-of-the-spirit discipleship discipleship-vs-leadership humility-and-service let-your-light-shine take-up-your-cross the-wise-builder the-narrow-way christianity-and-politics render-unto-caesar great-commission""".split()
ref_re = re.compile(r'\(([^)]+)\)\.?\s*$')
for slug in SPINE:
    t = (ROOT/f"{slug}.md").read_text()
    kc = t.split("key_concept: |")[1].split("\nexamples:")[0] if "key_concept: |" in t else ""
    lines = [l for l in kc.splitlines() if l.strip() and not l.strip().startswith("#")]
    print(f"=== {slug} ===")
    for l in lines[:8]:
        print(l[2:] if l.startswith("  ") else l)
    print()
PY
```

```
=== grace ===
For God so loved the world, that He gave His only begotten Son, that whoever believes in Him shall not perish, but have eternal life. (John 3:16).
[[Mercy]] withholds what I deserve; grace gives what I couldn't earn.
But God, being rich in mercy, because of His great love with which He loved us, even when we were dead in our transgressions, made us alive together with Christ (by grace you have been saved). (Ephesians 2:4-5). [[Free Grace]] is grace applied to eternal life: Christ paid what I couldn't; I receive by faith, not wages. [[Justification]] names the verdict grace triggers - declared righteous at faith. [[Sanctification]] and [[Grateful Obedience]] are grace after the gift: grow and obey from thanks, not to buy acceptance. [[Forgiveness]] flows the same way - forgive because grace already cleared my debt. [[Assurance]] and [[Standing vs Fellowship]] keep a bad week from collapsing standing with closeness. PKM mirror: [[Minimum Effective Dose]] - faith is the smallest act that still works for salvation; grace is why one sufficient gift beats a lifetime merit ladder.
Grace is gift I never earned starting with salvation and continuing through the walk after, not wages I could stack.
Grace and forgiveness flow the same way - gift received first, extension to others second.

=== mercy ===
Blessed are the merciful, for they shall receive mercy. (Matthew 5:7).
[[Grace]] gives good I couldn't earn; mercy spares bad I had coming. But God, being rich in mercy, because of His great love with which He loved us, even when we were dead in our transgressions, made us alive together with Christ (by grace you have been saved) (Ephesians 2:4-5). [[Free Grace]] is how that pair lands on eternal life - gift through faith, wrath withheld, heaven given. [[Forgiveness]] is mercy toward a debtor: release what you're owed because the King already cleared yours. [[The Beatitudes]] promise mercy to the merciful. [[Judge Not]] starts from the same ground - I needed mercy
… (output truncated)
```

import importlib.util, re

m = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)

```bash
explains = m.load_explanations()
slug_items = explains["grace"]

# clean input
kc = """  For God so loved the world, that He gave His only begotten Son, that whoever believes in Him shall not perish, but have eternal life. (John 3:16).

  [[Mercy]] withholds what I deserve; grace gives what I couldn't earn.
"""

while i < len(lines):

    print(f"i={i} raw={raw!r}")
    if not raw.startswith("  ") or not m.REF_ONLY.search(raw):
        out.append(raw)
        i += 1

    stripped = raw[2:]
    explain = m.find_explain(slug_items, stripped)

            continue

        print(f"  appended explain")
print("OUT:", repr("\n".join(out)))
```

```bash
  [[Mercy]] withholds what I deserve; grace gives what I couldn't earn.
"""
lines = kc.splitlines()
out = []
i = 0
while i < len(lines):
    raw = lines[i]
    print(f"i={i} raw={raw!r}")
    if not raw.startswith("  ") or not m.REF_ONLY.search(raw):
        out.append(raw)
        i += 1
        continue
    stripped = raw[2:]
    explain = m.find_explain(slug_items, stripped)
    ex_preview = (explain[:60] + '...') if explain else None
    print(f"  explain={ex_preview}")
    out.append(raw.rstrip())
    i += 1
    if explain:
        nxt = lines[i].strip() if i < len(lines) else ""
        print(f"  nxt={nxt!r}")
        if nxt and m.is_explain_line(lines[i], explain):
            print("  skip: is_explain_line")
            continue
        if nxt and not nxt.startswith("[[") and not nxt.startswith("|") and not m.REF_ONLY.search(nxt):
            print("  skip: already has gloss")
            continue
        out.append("")
        out.append(f"  {explain}")
        print("  appended explain")
print("OUT:", repr("\n".join(out)))
PY
```

```
i=0 raw='  For God so loved the world, that He gave His only begotten Son, that whoever believes in Him shall not perish, but have eternal life. (John 3:16).'
  explain=God didn't wait for me to earn it - He gave His Son so belie...
  nxt=''
  appended explain
i=1 raw=''
i=2 raw='  .'
i=3 raw=''
i=4 raw="  [[Mercy]] withholds what I deserve; grace gives what I couldn't earn."
OUT: "  For God so loved the world, that He gave His only begotten Son, that whoever believes in Him shall not perish, but have eternal life. (John 3:16).\n\n  God didn't wait for me to earn it - He gave His Son so belief, not merit, decides where I spend forever.\n\n  .\n\n  [[Mercy]] withholds what I deserve; grace gives what I couldn't earn."
```

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import importlib.util
spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("ave", "/home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com/scripts/apply-verse-explanations.py")
m = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
spec.loader.exec_module(m)
explains = m.load_explanations()
line = "Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called sons of God. (Matthew 5:9)."
ex = m.find_explain(explains["peacemakers"], line)
print(repr(ex))

# trace peacemakers kc
kc = """  Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called sons of God. (Matthew 5:9).

  Peacemaking is work: reconciliation, truth spoken in love, de-escalation when others want a fight. The blessing ties peacemaking to family resemblance - acting like the Father who reconciled me to Himself through [[Free Grace]] before I reconciled with anyone.
"""
lines = kc.splitlines()
out = []
i = 0
slug_items = explains["peacemakers"]
while i < len(lines):
    raw = lines[i]
    print(f"i={i} raw={raw[:60]!r}...")
    if not raw.startswith("  ") or not m.REF_ONLY.search(raw):
        out.append(raw)
        i += 1
        continue
    stripped = raw[2:]
    explain = m.find_explain(slug_items, stripped)
    print(f"  explain={repr(explain)[:80]}")
    out.append(raw.rstrip())
    i += 1
    if explain:
        nxt = lines[i].strip() if i < len(lines) else ""
        print(f"  nxt={nxt!r}")
        if nxt and m.is_explain_line(lines[i], explain):
            print("  skip is_explain")
            continue
        if nxt and not nxt.startswith("[[") and not m.REF_ONLY.search(nxt) and not nxt.startswith("|"):
            print("  skip already gloss")
            continue
        out.append("")
        out.append(f"  {explain}")
        print(f"  appended: {repr(f'  {explain}')[:80]}")
print("---")
for j,l in enumerate(out):
    print(j, repr(l))
PY
```

]:

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import re
REF_TAIL = re.compile(r"^(?P<head>.*\([^)]+\))\.?\s*(?P<tail>.+)$")
for s in [
    "Blessed are the peacemakers, for they shall be called sons of God. (Matthew 5:9).",
    "For God so loved the world, that He gave His only begotten Son, that whoever believes in Him shall not perish, but have eternal life. (John 3:16).",
    "For by grace you have been saved through faith; and that not of yourselves, it is the gift of God; not as a result of works, so that no one may boast. (Ephesians 2:8-9). Faith credited as righteousness for the one who believes (Romans 4:5).",
]:
    m = REF_TAIL.match(s)
    print(s[:50], "...")
    if m:
        print("  head:", m.group("head")[-40:])
        print("  tail:", repr(m.group("tail")))
    else:
        print("  NO MATCH")
PY
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The regex `REF_TAIL` incorrectly matches the trailing period after `(Ref)`. This breaks verse separation.

The period becomes a standalone verse entry, so the explanation never gets inserted. The fix requires tightening the `REF_TAIL` pattern so it does not match bare punctuation.

A better approach is to split only when substantive commentary follows `(Ref).`, rather than splitting on any trailing period. This avoids treating the period as a separate verse entry.

The new `SPLIT_AFTER_REF` pattern requires whitespace after the closing parenthesis, so it correctly skips lone trailing periods while still splitting verses from commentary. The plan is to preprocess and strip any stray `  .` lines, replace `REF_TAIL` with this stricter regex, handle multiple references per line via `finditer`, and insert explanations right after each verse segment.

Drafting helper functions for the verse-splitting logic. `normalize_ref` cleans reference strings. `lookup_explain` finds the correct explanation by matching against slugs, with a fallback fuzzy search. `extract_verse_chunks` will parse the text into verse segments.

`split_verses_from_line` now uses `finditer` to locate every `\(([^)]+)\)\.` pattern in a line. Each match yields a verse chunk plus its reference. Any remaining text after the last match is treated as commentary without a reference.

Empty or lone-period rest after a reference match is treated as commentary. Each verse chunk gets a period if missing, followed by a blank line and its explanation. Commentary-only lines pass through unchanged.
</details></think>

The second run duplicated glosses.

Rewriting the script to match references by slug only at the end of book references. Adding idempotency checks to skip verses that already have glosses. Running a cleanup pass to remove corrupted notes.

The reference matcher now scans for slugs and captures the full span ending at `).`. Overlapping matches are deduplicated, keeping only the longest span. A NASB edge case was noted where the reference text lacks a period inside the parentheses.

The grace reference text contains no period after "saved", so the first pass split it onto its own line. The correct placement is to attach both the grace note and the verse reference to the same preceding line.

The grace note gained a trailing period during the first pass, causing the matcher to falsely trigger on the phrase `(by grace you have been saved).`. The fix is to restrict reference matching to known book slugs and a strict scripture-book regex, preventing stray periods from creating false matches.

The script now uses a strict `BOOK_REF` regex to split verse chunks and inline commentary. Idempotency checks scan the following non-empty lines to skip verses that already carry a gloss. Post-processing collapses extra blank lines in the key concept section.

Consecutive identical gloss lines will also be deduplicated. Restoring the notes from git is impractical because the broken state is mixed with other good changes. The plan is to fix the script and run it, then manually clean up the worst cases.

A `dedupe_block` helper will strip bogus gloss lines and collapse consecutive blank lines. Before appending a new gloss, the script will scan the next three lines to avoid duplicates.

The deduplication logic now skips consecutive identical non-reference, non-wikilink lines. Three specific files—grace.md, free-grace.md, and forgiveness.md—will be fixed manually for structural issues.

The regex now explicitly excludes stray periods, so `(by grace you have been saved).` will not match. It still catches valid references like `(Ephesians 2:4-5).` and `(Romans 4:5).` However, comma-separated verse ranges such as `(Matthew 6:14-15, 18:21-35).` may fail to match.

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import re
from pathlib import Path
import yaml

ROOT = Path("/home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com")
NOTES = ROOT / "content/english/notes"
BOOK_REF_END = re.compile(
    r"\((?:(?:[1-3]\s+)?(?:Matthew|Mark|Luke|John|Romans|Ephesians|Galatians|"
    r"Deuteronomy|1 Corinthians|2 Corinthians|James|Hebrews|Psalm|Psalms|Proverbs)\s+[\d:,\s-]+)\)\."
)
explains = yaml.safe_load((ROOT / "data/ep-verse-explanations.yaml").read_text())["verses"]
issues = []
for slug in explains:
    path = NOTES / f"{slug}.md"
    text = path.read_text()
    start = text.index("key_concept: |\n") + len("key_concept: |\n")
    end = text.find("\nexamples:", start)
    lines = text[start:end].splitlines()
    for i, ln in enumerate(lines):
        if ln.strip() == ".":
            issues.append(f"{slug}:{i+1} lone period")
        if BOOK_REF_END.search(ln):
            # next non-empty should be gloss (not wikilink, not another ref line)
            j = i + 1
            while j < len(lines) and not lines[j].strip():
                j += 1
            if j >= len(lines):
                issues.append(f"{slug}:{i+1} verse missing gloss")
                continue
            nxt = lines[j].strip()
            if nxt.startswith("[[") or BOOK_REF_END.search(nxt):
                issues.append(f"{slug}:{i+1} verse not followed by gloss -> {nxt[:50]}")
print(f"{len(issues)} issues")
for x in issues[:40]:
    print(x)
PY
```

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import re
from pathlib import Path
import yaml

ROOT = Path("/home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com")
NOTES = ROOT / "content/english/notes"
BOOK_REF_END = re.compile(
    r"\((?:(?:[1-3]\s+)?(?:Matthew|Mark|Luke|John|Romans|Ephesians|Galatians|"
    r"Deuteronomy|1 Corinthians|2 Corinthians|James|Hebrews|Psalm|Psalms|Proverbs)\s+[\d:,\s-]+)\)\."
)
explains = yaml.safe_load((ROOT / "data/ep-verse-explanations.yaml").read_text())["verses"]
issues = []
dups = []
for slug in explains:
    path = NOTES / f"{slug}.md"
    text = path.read_text()
    start = text.index("key_concept: |\n") + len("key_concept: |\n")
    end = text.find("\nexamples:", start)
    lines = text[start:end].splitlines()
    seen = set()
    for i, ln in enumerate(lines):
        s = ln.strip()
        if s == ".":
            issues.append(f"{slug}:{i+1} lone period")
        if s and not BOOK_REF_END.search(s) and not s.startswith("[[") and not s.startswith("|"):
            if s in seen:
                dups.append(f"{slug}: duplicate '{s[:50]}'")
            seen.add(s)
        if BOOK_REF_END.search(ln):
            j = i + 1
            while j < len(lines) and not lines[j].strip():
                j += 1
            if j >= len(lines):
                issues.append(f"{slug}:{i+1} verse missing gloss")
                continue
            nxt = lines[j].strip()
            if nxt.startswith("[[") or BOOK_REF_END.search(nxt):
                issues.append(f"{slug}:{i+1} verse not followed by gloss")
print(f"{len(issues)} verse issues, {len(dups)} duplicate lines")
for x in issues + dups[:10]:
    print(x)
PY
```

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import re
from pathlib import Path
import yaml

ROOT = Path("/home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com")
NOTES = ROOT / "content/english/notes"
BOOK_REF_END = re.compile(
    r"\((?:(?:[1-3]\s+)?(?:Matthew|Mark|Luke|John|Romans|Ephesians|Galatians|"
    r"Deuteronomy|1 Corinthians|2 Corinthians|James|Hebrews|Psalm|Psalms|Proverbs)\s+[\d:,\s-]+)\)\."
)
explains = yaml.safe_load((ROOT / "data/ep-verse-explanations.yaml").read_text())["verses"]
issues, dups = [], []
for slug in explains:
    path = NOTES / f"{slug}.md"
    text = path.read_text()
    start = text.index("key_concept: |\n") + len("key_concept: |\n")
    end = text.find("\nexamples:", start)
    lines = text[start:end].splitlines()
    seen = set()
    for i, ln in enumerate(lines):
        s = ln.strip()
        if s == ".":
            issues.append(f"{slug}:{i+1} lone period")
        if s and not BOOK_REF_END.search(s) and not s.startswith("[[") and not s.startswith("|"):
            if s in seen:
                dups.append(f"{slug}: '{s[:55]}'")
            seen.add(s)
        if BOOK_REF_END.search(ln):
            j = i + 1
            while j < len(lines) and not lines[j].strip():
                j += 1
            if j >= len(lines):
                issues.append(f"{slug}:{i+1} missing gloss")
            else:
                nxt = lines[j].strip()
                if nxt.startswith("[[") or BOOK_REF_END.search(nxt):
                    issues.append(f"{slug}:{i+1} no gloss after verse")
print(f"{len(issues)} verse issues, {len(dups)} dup lines")
for x in issues + dups[:5]:
    print(x)
PY
```

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import re
from pathlib import Path
import yaml
ROOT = Path("/home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com")
NOTES = ROOT / "content/english/notes"
BOOK_REF_END = re.compile(r"\((?:(?:[1-3]\s+)?(?:Matthew|Mark|Luke|John|Romans|Ephesians|Galatians|Deuteronomy|1 Corinthians|2 Corinthians|James|Hebrews|Psalm|Psalms|Proverbs)\s+[\d:,\s-]+)\)\.")
explains = yaml.safe_load((ROOT / "data/ep-verse-explanations.yaml").read_text())["verses"]
issues = []
for slug in explains:
    text = (NOTES / f"{slug}.md").read_text()
    start = text.index("key_concept: |\n") + len("key_concept: |\n")
    end = text.find("\nexamples:", start)
    lines = text[start:end].splitlines()
    for i, ln in enumerate(lines):
        if ln.strip() == ".": issues.append(f"{slug} lone period")
        if BOOK_REF_END.search(ln):
            j = i + 1
            while j < len(lines) and not lines[j].strip(): j += 1
            if j >= len(lines) or lines[j].strip().startswith("[[") or BOOK_REF_END.search(lines[j]):
                issues.append(f"{slug} line {i+1}")
print("issues:", len(issues), issues)
```

```6:14:content/english/notes/grace.md
  For God so loved the world, that He gave His only begotten Son, that whoever believes in Him shall not perish, but have eternal life. (John 3:16).

  God didn't wait for me to earn it - He gave His Son so belief, not merit, decides where I spend forever.

  [[Mercy]] withholds what I deserve; grace gives what I couldn't earn.

  But God, being rich in mercy, because of His great love with which He loved us, even when we were dead in our transgressions, made us alive together with Christ (by grace you have been saved). (Ephesians 2:4-5).

  Paul names the same rescue from the dead side - alive with Christ by grace, not wages I stacked.
```

```bash
cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 scripts/apply-verse-explanations.py && python3 << 'PY'
import re
from pathlib import Path
import yaml
ROOT = Path("/home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com")
NOTES = ROOT / "content/english/notes"
BOOK = re.compile(r"\((?:(?:[1-3]\s+)?(?:Matthew|Mark|Luke|John|Romans|Ephesians|Galatians|Deuteronomy|1 Corinthians|2 Corinthians|James|Hebrews|Psalm|Psalms|Proverbs)\s+[\d:,\s-]+)(?:\s+NASB1995)?\)\.")
slugs = yaml.safe_load((ROOT/"data/ep-verse-explanations.yaml").read_text())["verses"]
missing_nasb, missing_gloss = [], []
for slug in slugs:
    text = (NOTES/f"{slug}.md").read_text()
    start = text.index("key_concept: |\n")+len("key_concept: |\n")
    end = text.find("\nexamples:", start)
    lines = text[start:end].splitlines()
    for i, ln in enumerate(lines):
        if BOOK.search(ln) and "NASB1995" not in ln:
            missing_nasb.append(f"{slug}:{i+1}")
        if BOOK.search(ln):
            j=i+1
            while j<len(lines) and not lines[j].strip(): j+=1
            if j>=len(lines) or lines[j].strip().startswith("[[") or BOOK.search(lines[j]):
                missing_gloss.append(f"{slug}:{i+1}")
print("missing NASB1995:", len(missing_nasb))
print("missing gloss:", len(missing_gloss))
for x in (missing_nasb+missing_gloss)[:15]: print(x)
PY
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Checking notes/single.html to understand how content is rendered.

Four approaches are being weighed. CSS alone cannot reliably detect Jesus references. Build-time script or Hugo render hooks could wrap verses in styled spans. Client-side JavaScript could scan for Gospel citations and color them red.

Only direct quotes attributed to Jesus should be colored red. Narrative passages and indirect references such as John 3:16 must remain unchanged. Paul’s quotations should also be excluded.

A simple book filter is insufficient. John 3:16 is narrator text, not a direct quote from Jesus. Only verses explicitly marked as Jesus’ words should be colored red. Two possible solutions are being weighed: adding YAML markers to identify direct quotes, or using the existing apply-jesus-quotes.py list as a heuristic.

Red-letter Bibles typically color Jesus’ direct speech throughout the Gospels and sometimes Revelation. The scope may therefore extend beyond the four Gospels to all direct quotes of Jesus in scripture. A `speaker: jesus` field in the scripture YAML could mark these passages during normalization. Alternatively, the existing apply-jesus-quotes.py list could serve as a heuristic. One unresolved question is whether to follow the traditional red-letter convention and color John 3:16, since it is narrator text rather than a direct quote from Jesus.

The user’s phrase “words of jesus” means only direct speech should be colored red. John 3:16 is author commentary, not Jesus’ own words, so it must stay black. The practical plan is to add a `speaker: jesus` flag to the scripture YAML entries that contain Jesus’ direct quotes.

The normalize script would wrap Jesus quotes in `<span class="jesus-words">` tags, with CSS applying the red color. A simpler shortcut is being considered: treat all Gospel citations as Jesus’ words, since the EP already curates those quotes. The user’s phrasing suggests a site-wide rule for bible quotations. The fastest implementation is now being outlined.
</details></think>

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

Gospel quotes now render in red automatically at build time.

**How it works:** When a note paragraph ends with a Gospel citation in the `(Matthew|Mark|Luke|John … NASB1995).` format, Hugo wraps the quote in `<span class="jesus-words">`. Paul and other writers (Romans, Ephesians, Galatians, etc.) stay the default text color.

**Changes:**
- `layouts/_partials/notes/render-content.html` — red-letter markup for Gospel NASB paragraphs
- `assets/css/custom.css` — `--color-jesus-words` (`#b91c1c` light / `#f87171` dark)

On `faith-and-works`, the Matthew 7:24–27 line is red; on `free-grace`, John 5:24 is red and Ephesians/Romans lines are not.

**Scope:** Standalone NASB verse paragraphs in notes `key_concept` (and anything else through `render-content.html`). Shareable thoughts, flashcards, and inline refs without the `NASB1995` suffix are unchanged.

If you want red letter everywhere — including flashcards, shareable thoughts, and partial inline quotes — say the word and we can extend it.

import re

    r&#34;Deuteronomy|1 Corinthians|2 Corinthians|James|Hebrews|Psalm|Psalms|Proverbs)\s+[\d:,\s-]+))&#34;

slugs = list(yaml.safe_load((ROOT/&#34;data/ep-verse-explanations.yaml&#34;).read_text())[&#34;verses&#34;].keys())

def lookup(ref):
    if ref in nasb: return ref
    best = None
    for k in nasb:
        if ref == k or ref.startswith(k+&#34;:&#34;) or k.startswith(ref):
            if not best or len(k) &gt; len(best): best = k
    return best

    text = (NOTES/f&#34;{slug}.md&#34;).read_text()
    if &#34;key_concept: |\n&#34; not in text: continue
    start = text.index(&#34;key_concept: |\n&#34;) + len(&#34;key_concept: |\n&#34;)
    end = text.find(&#34;\nexamples:&#34;, start)
    kc = text[start:end]
    for i, ln in enumerate(kc.splitlines(), 1):
        if not ln.startswith(&#34;  &#34;): continue
        s = ln[2:].strip()
        if not BOOK.search(s): continue
        # standalone if line is mostly the NASB text + ref
        refs = list(BOOK.finditer(s))
        body = BOOK.sub(&#34;&#34;, s).strip(&#34; .&#34;)
        for m in refs:
            ref = m.group(&#34;ref&#34;)
            key = lookup(ref)
            if not key: continue
            canon = nasb[key]
            norm = lambda x: re.sub(r&#34;[^a-z0-9]+&#34;, &#34;&#34;, x.lower())
            if norm(canon)[:30] in norm(body) or norm(body)[:30] in norm(canon) or (len(body)&lt;20 and not body.startswith(&#34;[[&#34;)):
                if len(refs)==1 and len(s) &lt; len(canon)+40:
                    continue  # likely standalone
            if &#34;[[ &#34; in s or (len(body)&gt;40 and norm(canon)[:25] not in norm(body)):
                inline.append((slug, i, ref, s[:70]))

print(f&#34;{len(inline)} likely inline mentions&#34;)

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import re
from pathlib import Path
import yaml

ROOT = Path("/home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com")
NOTES = ROOT / "content/english/notes"
BOOK = re.compile(
    r"\((?P<ref>(?:(?:[1-3]\s+)?(?:Matthew|Mark|Luke|John|Romans|Ephesians|Galatians|"
    r"Deuteronomy|1 Corinthians|2 Corinthians|James|Hebrews|Psalm|Psalms|Proverbs)\s+[\d:,\s-]+))"
    r"(?:\s+NASB1995)?\)\.?"
)
nasb = {k: v["text"] if isinstance(v, dict) else v 
        for k, v in yaml.safe_load((ROOT/"data/scripture-nasb1995.yaml").read_text())["verses"].items()}
slugs = list(yaml.safe_load((ROOT/"data/ep-verse-explanations.yaml").read_text())["verses"].keys())

def lookup(ref):
    if ref in nasb: return ref
    best = None
    for k in nasb:
        if ref == k or ref.startswith(k+":") or k.startswith(ref):
            if not best or len(k) > len(best): best = k
    return best

inline = []
for slug in slugs:
    text = (NOTES/f"{slug}.md").read_text()
    if "key_concept: |\n" not in text: continue
    start = text.index("key_concept: |\n") + len("key_concept: |\n")
    end = text.find("\nexamples:", start)
    kc = text[start:end]
    for i, ln in enumerate(kc.splitlines(), 1):
        if not ln.startswith("  "): continue
        s = ln[2:].strip()
        if not BOOK.search(s): continue
        # standalone if line is mostly the NASB text + ref
        refs = list(BOOK.finditer(s))
        body = BOOK.sub("", s).strip(" .")
        for m in refs:
            ref = m.group("ref")
            key = lookup(ref)
            if not key: continue
            canon = nasb[key]
            norm = lambda x: re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "", x.lower())
            if norm(canon)[:30] in norm(body) or norm(body)[:30] in norm(canon) or (len(body)<20 and not body.startswith("[[")):
                if len(refs)==1 and len(s) < len(canon)+40:
                    continue  # likely standalone
            if "[[ " in s or (len(body)>40 and norm(canon)[:25] not in norm(body)):
                inline.append((slug, i, ref, s[:70]))

print(f"{len(inline)} likely inline mentions")
for x in inline[:25]:
    print(f"  {x[0]}:{x[1]} ({x[2]}) {x[3]}...")
PY
```

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import re
from pathlib import Path
import yaml

ROOT = Path("/home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com")
NOTES = ROOT / "content/english/notes"
BOOK = re.compile(
    r"\((?:(?:[1-3]\s+)?(?:Matthew|Mark|Luke|John|Romans|Ephesians|Galatians|"
    r"Deuteronomy|1 Corinthians|2 Corinthians|James|Hebrews|Psalm|Psalms|Proverbs)\s+[\d:,\s-]+)\)"
)
slugs = list(yaml.safe_load((ROOT/"data/ep-verse-explanations.yaml").read_text())["verses"].keys())
for field in ["key_concept", "examples", "shareable_thought"]:
    hits = []
    for slug in slugs:
        text = (NOTES/f"{slug}.md").read_text()
        if f"{field}:" not in text: continue
        if field == "key_concept":
            start = text.index("key_concept: |\n") + len("key_concept: |\n")
            end = text.find("\nexamples:", start)
            block = text[start:end]
        else:
            m = re.search(rf"^{field}:\n((?:  - .*\n)+)", text, re.M)
            if not m: continue
            block = m.group(1)
        for i, ln in enumerate(block.splitlines(), 1):
            for ref_m in BOOK.finditer(ln):
                full = ref_m.group(0)
                if "NASB1995" not in ln[ref_m.end():ref_m.end()+12]:
                    hits.append((slug, field, i, full, ln.strip()[:75]))
    print(f"{field}: {len(hits)} refs without NASB1995")
    for h in hits[:12]:
        print(f"  {h[0]} {h[3]} | {h[4]}")
PY
```

```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import re
from pathlib import Path
import yaml

ROOT = Path("/home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com")
NOTES = ROOT / "content/english/notes"
NASB = {k: v["text"] if isinstance(v, dict) else v 
        for k, v in yaml.safe_load((ROOT/"data/scripture-nasb1995.yaml").read_text())["verses"].items()}
slugs = list(yaml.safe_load((ROOT/"data/ep-verse-explanations.yaml").read_text())["verses"].keys())
BOOK_END = re.compile(r"\([^)]*NASB1995\)\.")

dupes, partials, broken = [], [], []
for slug in slugs:
    text = (NOTES/f"{slug}.md").read_text()
    start = text.index("key_concept: |\n") + len("key_concept: |\n")
    end = text.find("\nexamples:", start)
    lines = text[start:end].splitlines()
    seen_refs = []
    for i, ln in enumerate(lines, 1):
        if "NASB1995)" in ln:
            m = re.search(r"\(([^)]+ NASB1995)\)", ln)
            if m:
                ref = m.group(1).replace(" NASB1995","")
                if ref in seen_refs:
                    dupes.append((slug, i, ref))
                seen_refs.append(ref)
        else:
            s = ln.strip()
            if not s or s.startswith("[["): continue
            for key, canon in NASB.items():
                if len(canon) > 30 and canon[:35].lower() in s.lower() and "NASB1995" not in s:
                    partials.append((slug, i, key, s[:60]))
                    break
        if ln.strip() and not ln.startswith("  ") and ln.strip() != "":
            broken.append((slug, i, ln[:50]))
    # line ending mid-sentence without period
    for i, ln in enumerate(lines):
        s = ln[2:].strip() if ln.startswith("  ") else ""
        if s and not s.endswith(('.', '!', '?', ']')) and i+1 < len(lines) and lines[i+1].strip().startswith(";"):
            broken.append((slug, i+1, "split sentence"))

print("duplicate NASB paragraphs:", len(dupes))
for x in dupes[:15]: print(" ", x)
print("partial quotes in commentary:", len(partials))
for x in partials[:15]: print(" ", x)
print("broken:", len(broken))
for x in broken[:10]: print(" ", x)
PY
```

Paraphrase lines that repeat a canon without a reference will be dropped once the reference is already emitted. Broken lines will be merged when the previous line lacks a period and the next begins with a semicolon or lowercase fragment. Matthew 6:18 and Mark 12:31 will be added to the YAML.

Lines containing only a NASB reference will be emitted as verse paragraphs with empty bodies.
```bash
python3 << 'PY'
import re
from pathlib import Path
import yaml

ROOT = Path("/home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com")
NOTES = ROOT / "content/english/notes"
NASB = {k: v["text"] if isinstance(v, dict) else v 
        for k, v in yaml.safe_load((ROOT/"data/scripture-nasb1995.yaml").read_text())["verses"].items()}
slugs = list(yaml.safe_load((ROOT/"data/ep-verse-explanations.yaml").read_text())["verses"].keys())
BOOK = re.compile(r"\((?:(?:[1-3]\s+)?(?:Matthew|Mark|Luke|John|Romans|Ephesians|Galatians|Deuteronomy|1 Corinthians|2 Corinthians|James|Hebrews|Psalm|Psalms|Proverbs)\s+[^)]*)\)(?!\s*NASB1995)")
PAREN = re.compile(r"\([^)]*(?:Matthew|Mark|Luke|John|Romans|Ephesians)[^)]*\)")

issues = []
for slug in slugs:
    text = (NOTES/f"{slug}.md").read_text()
    start = text.index("key_concept: |\n") + len("key_concept: |\n")
    end = text.find("\nexamples:", start)
    lines = text[start:end].splitlines()
    seen = []
    for i, ln in enumerate(lines, 1):
        s = ln[2:] if ln.startswith("  ") else ln
        if BOOK.search(ln):
            issues.append((slug, i, "ref without NASB1995", ln.strip()[:70]))
        if "NASB1995)" in ln:
            m = re.search(r"\(([^)]+ NASB1995)\)", ln)
            if m:
                ref = m.group(1).replace(" NASB1995","")
                if ref in seen:
                    issues.append((slug, i, "duplicate verse", ref))
                seen.append(ref)
        elif PAREN.search(ln) and "[[" in ln:
            issues.append((slug, i, "inline ref in commentary", ln.strip()[:70]))

print(f"{len(issues)} issues")
for x in issues:
    print(f"  {x[0]}:{x[1]} {x[2]} | {x[3]}")
PY
```

<summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com &amp;&amp; python3 - &lt;&lt;&#39;PY&#39;
import re, yaml
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path(&#39;.&#39;)
EXPLAIN = yaml.safe_load((ROOT/&#39;data/ep-verse-explanations.yaml&#39;).read_text())[&#39;verses&#39;]
NOTES = ROOT/&#39;content/english/notes&#39;
BOOK = r&#34;(?:[1-3]\s+)?(?:Matthew|Mark|Luke|John|Romans|Ephesians|Galatians|Deuteronomy|1 Corinthians|2 Corinthians|James|Hebrews|Psalm|Psalms|Proverbs)&#34;
PAREN = re.compile(rf&#34;\(([^)]*{BOOK}[^)]*)\)&#34;)
NASB = re.compile(r&#39;NASB1995&#39;)

issues = []
for slug in EXPLAIN:
    p = NOTES/f&#39;{slug}.md&#39;
    if not p.exists():
        issues.append((slug, &#39;missing file&#39;))
        continue
    text = p.read_text()
    m = re.search(r&#39;key_concept:\s*\|\s*\n((?:  .*\n)*)&#39;, text)
    if not m:
        continue
    block = m.group(1)
    for i, line in enumerate(block.splitlines(), 1):
        s = line.strip()
        if not s: continue
        for pm in PAREN.finditer(s):
            inner = pm.group(1)
            if &#39;NASB1995&#39; not in inner and not NASB.search(s):
                # inline ref in commentary
                if not s.endswith(&#39;NASB1995).&#39;):
                    issues.append((slug, f&#39;inline ref: {inner!r} in: {s[:80]}...&#39;))
        # verse line should end NASB1995).
        if BOOK in s and &#39;NASB1995&#39; in s and not s.endswith(&#39;NASB1995).&#39;):
            issues.append((slug, f&#39;bad verse line format: {s[:80]}&#39;))

print(f&#39;{len(issues)} issues&#39;)
for slug, msg in sorted(issues):
    print(f&#39;  {slug}: {msg}&#39;)

```bash
import re, yaml
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path('.')
EXPLAIN = yaml.safe_load((ROOT/'data/ep-verse-explanations.yaml').read_text())['verses']
NOTES = ROOT/'content/english/notes'
BOOK = r"(?:[1-3]\s+)?(?:Matthew|Mark|Luke|John|Romans|Ephesians|Galatians|Deuteronomy|1 Corinthians|2 Corinthians|James|Hebrews|Psalm|Psalms|Proverbs)"
PAREN = re.compile(rf"\(([^)]*{BOOK}[^)]*)\)")
NASB = re.compile(r'NASB1995')

issues = []
for slug in EXPLAIN:
    p = NOTES/f'{slug}.md'
    if not p.exists():
        issues.append((slug, 'missing file'))
        continue
    text = p.read_text()
    m = re.search(r'key_concept:\s*\|\s*\n((?:  .*\n)*)', text)
    if not m:
        continue
    block = m.group(1)
    for i, line in enumerate(block.splitlines(), 1):
        s = line.strip()
        if not s: continue
        for pm in PAREN.finditer(s):
            inner = pm.group(1)
            if 'NASB1995' not in inner and not NASB.search(s):
                # inline ref in commentary
                if not s.endswith('NASB1995).'):
                    issues.append((slug, f'inline ref: {inner!r} in: {s[:80]}...'))
        # verse line should end NASB1995).
        if BOOK in s and 'NASB1995' in s and not s.endswith('NASB1995).'):
            issues.append((slug, f'bad verse line format: {s[:80]}'))

print(f'{len(issues)} issues')
for slug, msg in sorted(issues):
    print(f'  {slug}: {msg}')
PY
```

```bash
cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 - <<'PY'
import re, yaml
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path('.')
EXPLAIN = yaml.safe_load((ROOT/'data/ep-verse-explanations.yaml').read_text())['verses']
NOTES = ROOT/'content/english/notes'
BOOK_REF_END = re.compile(
    r"\((?:(?:[1-3]\s+)?(?:Matthew|Mark|Luke|John|Romans|Ephesians|Galatians|"
    r"Deuteronomy|1 Corinthians|2 Corinthians|James|Hebrews|Psalm|Psalms|Proverbs)\s+[\d:,\s-]+)"
    r"(?:\s+NASB1995)?\)\."
)

missing = []
for slug, items in EXPLAIN.items():
    p = NOTES/f'{slug}.md'
    if not p.exists(): continue
    text = p.read_text()
    m = re.search(r'key_concept:\s*\|\s*\n((?:  .*\n)*)', text)
    if not m: continue
    lines = m.group(1).splitlines()
    refs_in_yaml = [e['ref'] for e in items]
    i = 0
    while i < len(lines):
        s = lines[i].strip()
        if BOOK_REF_END.search(s):
            # find matching explain
            inner = BOOK_REF_END.search(s).group(0)[1:-2].replace(' NASB1995','')
            matched = None
            for r in sorted(refs_in_yaml, key=len, reverse=True):
                if inner == r or inner.startswith(r+':') or r in inner:
                    matched = r
                    break
            if matched:
                gloss = next(e['explain'] for e in items if e['ref']==matched)
                j = i+1
                while j < len(lines) and not lines[j].strip():
                    j += 1
                nxt = lines[j].strip() if j < len(lines) else ''
                if not nxt or nxt.startswith('[[') or BOOK_REF_END.search(nxt) or gloss[:24] not in nxt:
                    missing.append((slug, matched, gloss[:50]))
        i += 1

print(f'{len(missing)} missing glosses')
for slug, ref, gloss in missing:
    print(f'  {slug} / {ref}: {gloss}...')
PY
```

```bash
cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 - <<'PY'
import yaml
from pathlib import Path
from importlib.util import spec_from_loader, module_from_spec
import importlib.machinery
path = Path('scripts/apply-verse-explanations.py')
loader = importlib.machinery.SourceFileLoader('ave', path)
mod = module_from_spec(spec_from_loader('ave', loader)); loader.exec_module(mod)

explains = mod.load_explanations()
kc = Path('content/english/notes/love-your-neighbor.md').read_text()
start = kc.index('key_concept: |\n') + len('key_concept: |\n')
end = kc.find('\nexamples:', start)
old = kc[start:end]
new = mod.process_block(old, explains['love-your-neighbor'])
print('changed', old != new)
for a,b in zip(old.splitlines(), new.splitlines()):
    if a!=b: print('DIFF', repr(a), '->', repr(b))
# show lines around Luke
for i,l in enumerate(new.splitlines()):
    if 'Luke' in l or 'Samaritan' in l or 'Mercy' in l:
        print(i, repr(l))
```

from pathlib import Path

VERSE_LINE = re.compile(rf&#34;^.+\({BOOK}\s+[\d:,\s;-]+\s+NASB1995\)\.$&#34;)

        if VERS_LINE := VERS_LINE if False else VERS_LINE:

```bash
import re, yaml
```

```bash
import re, yaml
from pathlib import Path

ROOT = Path('.')
slugs = list(yaml.safe_load((ROOT/'data/ep-verse-explanations.yaml').read_text())['verses'])
NOTES = ROOT/'content/english/notes'
BOOK = r"(?:[1-3]\s+)?(?:Matthew|Mark|Luke|John|Romans|Ephesians|Galatians|Deuteronomy|1 Corinthians|2 Corinthians|James|Hebrews|Psalm|Psalms|Proverbs|Colossians|Philippians|1 Peter|2 Peter|1 John|2 Timothy|Hebrews|Revelation|Isaiah|Jeremiah)"
REF = re.compile(rf"\({BOOK}\s+[\d:,\s;-]+(?:\s+NASB1995)?\)")
VERSE_LINE = re.compile(rf"^.+\({BOOK}\s+[\d:,\s;-]+\s+NASB1995\)\.$")

inline, no_nasb, ok = [], [], 0
for slug in slugs:
    p = NOTES/f'{slug}.md'
    text = p.read_text()
    m = re.search(r'key_concept:\s*\|\s*\n((?:  .*\n)*)', text)
    if not m:
        continue
    for line in m.group(1).splitlines():
        s = line.strip()
        if not s: continue
        refs = REF.findall(s)
        if not refs: continue
        if VERSE_LINE.match(s):
            ok += 1
            if len(refs) > 1:
                inline.append((slug, 'multi-ref verse line', s[:90]))
            continue
        if 'NASB1995' in s and not s.endswith('NASB1995).'):
            no_nasb.append((slug, s[:100]))
        elif any('NASB1995' not in r for r in refs):
            inline.append((slug, 'inline ref in commentary', s[:100]))

print(f'verse paragraphs: {ok}')
print(f'inline issues: {len(inline)}')
for x in inline: print(' ', x)
print(f'bad format: {len(no_nasb)}')
for x in no_nasb: print(' ', x)
```


**What changed this round**

- **`merge_broken_lines`** — merges commentary split across blank lines (e.g. `faithful-steward`, `love-your-enemies`).
- **Manual cleanup** — removed broken partial quotes and duplicate Matthew 5:45 block in `love-your-enemies`.
- **`ep-verse-explanations.yaml`** — added glosses for `Mark 12:31`, `Matthew 6:18`, `Matthew 16:27`; dropped redundant `Matthew 5:45` entry.
- **Re-ran scripts** — `normalize-verse-paragraphs.py` then `apply-verse-explanations.py` (always in that order).

**Audit:** 0 inline scripture refs left in `key_concept` across the EP spine. Gospel quotes render with red-letter styling (`jesus-words`).

**Examples**

- `love-your-neighbor.md` — three separate paragraphs: Matthew 22:39, Mark 12:31, Luke 10:37, each with a gloss.
- `eternal-rewards.md` — four verses: Matthew 6:4, 6:18, 16:27, 25:21.
- `love-your-enemies.md` — one combined Matthew 5:44–45 paragraph (no duplicate 5:45).

**Not extended:** `shareable_thought` and `examples` still use short inline refs (130-char limit). Say if you want those normalized too.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The plan uses kjvstudy.org's red_letter_verses.json for verse references and KJV red letter text.

Full KJV and BSB verse text will come from helloao API endpoints. BSB uses USJ red letter markup. The database structure is still being decided between a single YAML file, separate KJV and BSB files, or JSON.

JSON is preferred over YAML for machine readability. The build script will download kjvstudy's red letter JSON, filter for the four gospels and Acts, fetch KJV and BSB verse text per chapter via helloao API, and store span details alongside full verse text in both translations.

The kjvstudy JSON contains KJV partial red text but lacks BSB equivalents. BSB red letter spans must be extracted from its USJ markup instead.
</details></think>

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: python3 - &lt;&lt;&#39;PY&#39;
import json, urllib.request, re
BOOKS = {&#39;Matthew&#39;:&#39;MAT&#39;,&#39;Mark&#39;:&#39;MRK&#39;,&#39;Luke&#39;:&#39;LUK&#39;,&#39;John&#39;:&#39;JHN&#39;,&#39;Acts&#39;:&#39;ACT&#39;}
# check BSB for wordsOfJesus in a few chapters
for trans in (&#39;BSB&#39;,&#39;eng_kjv&#39;):
    found = 0
    for book, bid in BOOKS.items():
        for ch in [3,5,7]:
            url = f&#39;https://bible.helloao.org/api/{trans}/{bid}/{ch}.json&#39;
            d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(url).read())
            for item in d[&#39;chapter&#39;][&#39;content&#39;]:
                if item.get(&#39;type&#39;)!=&#39;verse&#39;: continue
                def walk(x):
                    if isinstance(x, dict):
                        if x.get(&#39;wordsOfJesus&#39;): yield x
                        for v in x.values(): yield from walk(v)
                    elif isinstance(x, list):
                        for i in x: yield from walk(i)
                if list(walk(item.get(&#39;content&#39;,[]))):
                    found += 1
    print(trans, &#39;chapters with wordsOfJesus sample&#39;, found)
PY</summary>
```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
import json, urllib.request, re
BOOKS = {'Matthew':'MAT','Mark':'MRK','Luke':'LUK','John':'JHN','Acts':'ACT'}
# check BSB for wordsOfJesus in a few chapters
for trans in ('BSB','eng_kjv'):
    found = 0
    for book, bid in BOOKS.items():
        for ch in [3,5,7]:
            url = f'https://bible.helloao.org/api/{trans}/{bid}/{ch}.json'
            d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(url).read())
            for item in d['chapter']['content']:
                if item.get('type')!='verse': continue
                def walk(x):
                    if isinstance(x, dict):
                        if x.get('wordsOfJesus'): yield x
                        for v in x.values(): yield from walk(v)
                    elif isinstance(x, list):
                        for i in x: yield from walk(i)
                if list(walk(item.get('content',[]))):
                    found += 1
    print(trans, 'chapters with wordsOfJesus sample', found)
PY
```

```bash
cd /tmp && curl -sL 'https://github.com/BSB-publishing/bsb2usfm/releases/latest/download/BSB_usj.zip' -o BSB_usj.zip && unzip -q -o BSB_usj.zip -d bsb_usj && ls bsb_usj | head && python3 - <<'PY'
import json, glob
# find matthew file
files = glob.glob('/tmp/bsb_usj/**/*.json', recursive=True)
print('files', len(files), files[:5])
for fp in files:
    if 'MAT' in fp.upper() or 'Matthew' in fp or '40' in fp:
        print('mat', fp)
        break
else:

            print('mat2', fp); break
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The BSB USJ text marks Words of Jesus with the USFM marker `wj`.

I will build a script to extract `wj` spans from the BSB USJ files for the five books. For the KJV, I will use the helloao API's `eng_kjv` with its `wordsOfJesus` markup as the authoritative source instead of kjvstudy.

kjvstudy covers 1940 verses in the gospels and Acts. Each translation’s own red-letter markup will be used directly. This avoids forcing kjvstudy’s editorial choices.

The database will be a unified YAML structure. It will list the five books and both translations. Each verse will store the full text and the extracted red-letter words.

YAML is sufficient for the database size. I will create `scripts/build-jesus-words-db.py` and save the result to `data/jesus-words.yaml`. The script will walk the BSB USJ structure per book, tracking chapters and verses to collect text and `wj` spans. For the KJV, it will fetch all chapters for the five books from the helloao API and extract the `wordsOfJesus` markup.
</details></think>

```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
import json, urllib.request
BOOKS = {'Matthew':'MAT','Mark':'MRK','Luke':'LUK','John':'JHN','Acts':'ACT'}
chapters = {'Matthew':28,'Mark':16,'Luke':24,'John':21,'Acts':28}
count = 0
samples = []
for book, bid in BOOKS.items():
    for ch in range(1, chapters[book]+1):
        url = f'https://bible.helloao.org/api/eng_kjv/{bid}/{ch}.json'
        d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(url).read())
        for item in d['chapter']['content']:
            if item.get('type')!='verse': continue
            vnum = item['number']
            def extract(content):
                full=''; words=''
                for part in content:
                    if isinstance(part,str):
                        full += part
                        words += part
                    elif isinstance(part,dict):
                        t = part.get('text','')
                        full += t
                        if part.get('wordsOfJesus'): words += t
                return full.strip(), words.strip()
            full, words = extract(item['content'])
            if words:
                count += 1
                if len(samples)<3: samples.append((f'{book} {ch}:{vnum}', full[:80], words[:80]))
print('KJV jesus verses', count)
for s in samples: print(s)
```

```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
import json, urllib.request
BOOKS = {'Matthew':'MAT','Mark':'MRK','Luke':'LUK','John':'JHN','Acts':'ACT'}
chapters = {'Matthew':28,'Mark':16,'Luke':24,'John':21,'Acts':28}

def extract(content):
    full, words = [], []
    for part in content:
        if isinstance(part, str):
            full.append(part)
        elif isinstance(part, dict):
            t = part.get('text', '')
            if t:
                full.append(t)
                if part.get('wordsOfJesus'):
                    words.append(t)
    return ''.join(full).strip(), ''.join(words).strip()

count = 0
for book, bid in BOOKS.items():
    bc = 0
    for ch in range(1, chapters[book]+1):
        d = json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(f'https://bible.helloao.org/api/eng_kjv/{bid}/{ch}.json').read())
        for item in d['chapter']['content']:
            if item.get('type') != 'verse':
                continue
            full, words = extract(item['content'])
            if words:
                count += 1
                bc += 1
    print(book, bc)
print('total', count)
PY
```

import json
from pathlib import Path

BOOK_FILES = {
    &#39;Matthew&#39;: &#39;/tmp/bsb_usj/MAT.usj&#39;,
    &#39;Mark&#39;: &#39;/tmp/bsb_usj/MRK.usj&#39;,
    &#39;Luke&#39;: &#39;/tmp/bsb_usj/LUK.usj&#39;,
    &#39;John&#39;: &#39;/tmp/bsb_usj/JHN.usj&#39;,
    &#39;Acts&#39;: &#39;/tmp/bsb_usj/ACT.usj&#39;,
}

def walk(node, chapter=0, verse=0, full_parts=None, wj_parts=None, out=None):
    if full_parts is None: full_parts = []
    if wj_parts is None: wj_parts = []
    if out is None: out = {}

    if isinstance(node, dict):
        if node.get(&#39;type&#39;) == &#39;chapter&#39;:
            chapter = int(node.get(&#39;number&#39;, chapter))
            full_parts, wj_parts = [], []
        elif node.get(&#39;type&#39;) == &#39;verse&#39;:
            # flush previous verse
            if verse and full_parts:
                key = f&#34;{book} {chapter}:{verse}&#34;
                full = &#39;&#39;.join(full_parts).strip()
                words = &#39;&#39;.join(wj_parts).strip()
                if words:
                    out[key] = (full, words)
            verse = int(node.get(&#39;number&#39;, verse))
            full_parts, wj_parts = [], []
        elif node.get(&#39;type&#39;) == &#39;char&#39; and node.get(&#39;marker&#39;) == &#39;wj&#39;:
            text = flatten(node.get(&#39;content&#39;, []))
            wj_parts.append(text)
            full_parts.append(text)
            return chapter, verse, full_parts, wj_parts, out
        else:
            for k, v in node.items():
                if k == &#39;content&#39;:
                    for child in v:
                        chapter, verse, full_parts, wj_parts, out = walk(child, chapter, verse, full_parts, wj_parts, out)
    elif isinstance(node, list):
        for child in node:
            chapter, verse, full_parts, wj_parts, out = walk(child, chapter, verse, full_parts, wj_parts, out)
    elif isinstance(node, str):
        full_parts.append(node)
    return chapter, verse, full_parts, wj_parts, out

def flatten(content):
    parts = []
    for c in content:
        if isinstance(c, str):
            parts.append(c)
        elif isinstance(c, dict):
            parts.append(flatten(c.get(&#39;content&#39;, [])))
    return &#39;&#39;.join(parts)

for book, fp in BOOK_FILES.items():
    data = json.loads(Path(fp).read_text())
    # custom walk with book name
    out = {}
    chapter = verse = 0
    full_parts, wj_parts = [], []

    def process(node):
        nonlocal chapter, verse, full_parts, wj_parts
        if isinstance(node, dict):
            t = node.get(&#39;type&#39;)
            if t == &#39;chapter&#39;:
                if verse and full_parts:
                    save()
                chapter = int(node[&#39;number&#39;]); verse = 0; full_parts, wj_parts = [], []
            elif t == &#39;verse&#39;:
                if verse and full_parts:
                    save()
                verse = int(node[&#39;number&#39;]); full_parts, wj_parts = [], []
            elif t == &#39;char&#39; and node.get(&#39;marker&#39;) == &#39;wj&#39;:
                text = flatten(node.get(&#39;content&#39;, []))
                wj_parts.append(text); full_parts.append(text)
                return
            for child in node.get(&#39;content&#39;, []):
                process(child)
        elif isinstance(node, list):
            for child in node:
                process(child)
        elif isinstance(node, str):
            full_parts.append(node)

    def save():
        full = &#39;&#39;.join(full_parts).strip()
        words = &#39;&#39;.join(wj_parts).strip()
        if words:
            out[f&#39;{book} {chapter}:{verse}&#39;] = (full, words)

    process(data[&#39;content&#39;])
    if verse and full_parts:
        save()
    print(book, len(out))
    # sample
    for k in list(out)[:1]:
        print(&#39; sample&#39;, k, out[k][1][:60])

```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
import json
from pathlib import Path

BOOK_FILES = {
    'Matthew': '/tmp/bsb_usj/MAT.usj',
    'Mark': '/tmp/bsb_usj/MRK.usj',
    'Luke': '/tmp/bsb_usj/LUK.usj',
    'John': '/tmp/bsb_usj/JHN.usj',
    'Acts': '/tmp/bsb_usj/ACT.usj',
}

def walk(node, chapter=0, verse=0, full_parts=None, wj_parts=None, out=None):
    if full_parts is None: full_parts = []
    if wj_parts is None: wj_parts = []
    if out is None: out = {}

    if isinstance(node, dict):
        if node.get('type') == 'chapter':
            chapter = int(node.get('number', chapter))
            full_parts, wj_parts = [], []
        elif node.get('type') == 'verse':
            # flush previous verse
            if verse and full_parts:
                key = f"{book} {chapter}:{verse}"
                full = ''.join(full_parts).strip()
                words = ''.join(wj_parts).strip()
                if words:
                    out[key] = (full, words)
            verse = int(node.get('number', verse))
            full_parts, wj_parts = [], []
        elif node.get('type') == 'char' and node.get('marker') == 'wj':
            text = flatten(node.get('content', []))
            wj_parts.append(text)
            full_parts.append(text)
            return chapter, verse, full_parts, wj_parts, out
        else:
            for k, v in node.items():
                if k == 'content':
                    for child in v:
                        chapter, verse, full_parts, wj_parts, out = walk(child, chapter, verse, full_parts, wj_parts, out)
    elif isinstance(node, list):
        for child in node:
            chapter, verse, full_parts, wj_parts, out = walk(child, chapter, verse, full_parts, wj_parts, out)
    elif isinstance(node, str):
        full_parts.append(node)
    return chapter, verse, full_parts, wj_parts, out

def flatten(content):
    parts = []
    for c in content:
        if isinstance(c, str):
            parts.append(c)
        elif isinstance(c, dict):
            parts.append(flatten(c.get('content', [])))
    return ''.join(parts)

for book, fp in BOOK_FILES.items():
    data = json.loads(Path(fp).read_text())
    # custom walk with book name
    out = {}
    chapter = verse = 0
    full_parts, wj_parts = [], []

    def process(node):
        nonlocal chapter, verse, full_parts, wj_parts
        if isinstance(node, dict):
            t = node.get('type')
            if t == 'chapter':
                if verse and full_parts:
                    save()
                chapter = int(node['number']); verse = 0; full_parts, wj_parts = [], []
            elif t == 'verse':
                if verse and full_parts:
                    save()
                verse = int(node['number']); full_parts, wj_parts = [], []
            elif t == 'char' and node.get('marker') == 'wj':
                text = flatten(node.get('content', []))
                wj_parts.append(text); full_parts.append(text)
                return
            for child in node.get('content', []):
                process(child)
        elif isinstance(node, list):
            for child in node:
                process(child)
        elif isinstance(node, str):
            full_parts.append(node)

    def save():
        full = ''.join(full_parts).strip()
        words = ''.join(wj_parts).strip()
        if words:
            out[f'{book} {chapter}:{verse}'] = (full, words)

    process(data['content'])
    if verse and full_parts:
        save()
    print(book, len(out))
    # sample
    for k in list(out)[:1]:
        print(' sample', k, out[k][1][:60])
PY
```

```bash
python3 - <<'PY'
import json
from pathlib import Path
data = json.loads(Path('/tmp/bsb_usj/MAT.usj').read_text())
# dump chapter 3 content around v15
in_ch3 = False
for node in data['content']:
    if isinstance(node, dict) and node.get('type')=='chapter' and node.get('number')=='3':
        in_ch3 = True
    elif isinstance(node, dict) and node.get('type')=='chapter' and in_ch3:
        break
    if in_ch3:
        print(json.dumps(node, ensure_ascii=False)[:400])
        print('---')
PY
```

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

Built a red-letter database for **KJV** and **BSB** across **Matthew, Mark, Luke, John, and Acts**.

### Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `data/jesus-words.yaml` | **1,987 verse refs** with full verse text + Jesus-only words in both translations |
| `scripts/build-jesus-words-db.py` | Rebuild script (downloads BSB USJ, fetches KJV from helloao API) |

### Schema

Each entry looks like:

```yaml
Matthew 3:15:
  kjv:
    verse: "And Jesus answering said unto him, Suffer it to be so now…"
    words: "Suffer it to be so now: for thus it becometh us to fulfil all righteousness."
    full: false          # partial verse — only `words` is red letter
  bsb:
    verse: "“Let it be so now,” Jesus replied…"
    words: "“Let it be so now,” “It is fitting for us to fulfill all righteousness in this way.”"
    full: false
```

- **`verse`** — full verse text  
- **`words`** — Jesus' words only (red letter)  
- **`full: true`** — entire verse is Jesus speaking  

### Sources

- **KJV** — [helloao `eng_kjv` API](https://bible.helloao.org/api/eng_kjv) with native `wordsOfJesus` markup (public domain in the US)
- **BSB** — [BSB USJ](https://github.com/BSB-publishing/bsb2usfm) with native `wj` (Words of Jesus) markers (public domain)

Each translation uses its **own** red-letter editorial choices, so counts differ slightly (1,963 KJV vs 1,976 BSB).

### Rebuild

```bash
python3 scripts/build-jesus-words-db.py
```

BSB USJ is cached under `.cache/jesus-words/` (gitignored). KJV chapters are fetched live (~90s).

Acts is included — e.g. `Acts 20:35` has the partial quote *"It is more blessed to give than to receive."* in both translations.
